title: Capability security — per-process capability tokens
purpose: B-049 established capability-gated imports at the module level. This brief extends capabilities to the process level — each process has a set of capability tokens that determine what operations it can perform. A supervisor can spawn a child with restricted capabilities (no file I/O, no network, no spawn).
task: Add per-process capability sets and enforcement at NIF call boundaries.

requirements.title: CapabilitySet on Process
requirements.spec: Each process carries a set of capabilities that gate what operations it can perform.
acceptance: CapabilitySet is a bitfield or HashSet<Capability>
spawn/spawn_link/spawn_opt accept a capabilities option to restrict child
Test: spawn child with restricted capabilities, verify restrictions apply

requirements.title: NIF capability enforcement
requirements.spec: NIF calls check the calling process's capabilities before execution.
acceptance: Each NativeEntry has a required_capability field
Missing capability returns {error, capability_denied} not badarg
Test: call file:read with FileIO capability denied → capability_denied error

requirements.title: Capability attenuation (never escalate)
requirements.spec: A process can only grant capabilities it has — capabilities can be attenuated (reduced) but never escalated.
acceptance: spawn with capabilities checks caller has all requested capabilities
Attempting to grant a capability the caller doesn't have returns badarg
Test: process with {net, file} spawns child with {file} only — allowed. Spawns child with {net, file, spawn} — denied (spawn not in parent set)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement capability revocation after spawn — capabilities are fixed at creation
SHALL NOT implement capability delegation to other processes — only parent-to-child at spawn
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
